title: Hierarchy of Key Concepts in Group Theory
description: A visual breakdown showing how foundational algebraic structures build up to the formal definition of a Group.
relationships: A Set is equipped with a Binary Operation
Closure is a property checked on the Set under the Operation
Associativity is a property of the Operation
The Identity Element must exist within the Set
Every Element in the Set must have an Inverse Element within the Set
A GROUP is formally defined when a Set and its Operation satisfy all four axioms: Closure, Associativity, Identity, and Inverses.
